name: Lina Sun
bio: Lina will join the RCV Lab in Fall 2025 to focus on robust multi-sensor perception for mobile robots. She previously interned on visual-inertial mapping projects and enjoys deploying research code onto field robots.
research: Multimodal localization in dense urban campuses
Event and RGB fusion for long-horizon SLAM
Trustworthy perception for autonomous inspection platforms
publications: Sun, L., et al. "Robust Polarimetric SLAM for Dynamic Campuses." 2025. [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.01234)

name: Ravi Patel
bio: Ravi joins the lab from an embedded systems background and is interested in data-efficient manipulation. He prototypes robotic hands and pipelines that connect perception with grasp synthesis.
research: Learning-based grasp planning under limited supervision
Tactile and vision fusion for dexterous manipulation
Hardware-aware policy deployment on mobile manipulators
publications: Patel, R., et al. "Lightweight Grasp Policies for Mobile Manipulators." ICRA Workshop, 2024. [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.10011)

name: Meiling Chen
bio: Meiling is joining as a master's student to explore human-robot interaction for service robots. She has built interactive perception demos and leads outreach workshops for younger students.
research: Evaluation of user trust in assistive robots
publications: Chen, M., et al. "Interactive Perception Benchmarks for Service Robots." Under review, 2025. [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.04567)

name: Haoran Zhou
bio: Haoran is an undergraduate researcher who supports embedded perception projects. He maintains lab dashboards and enjoys rapid prototyping with Jetson boards.
research: Efficient stereo depth on edge devices
Telemetry and health monitoring for field robots
Dataset tooling for labeling and QA
publications: Zhou, H., et al. "Edge-Friendly Stereo Pipelines for Field Robotics." Student Technical Report, 2024. [[Paper]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.05521)
